name: Italian Fish Soup
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Fish stock or water or clam nectar
1 lb White fish (boneless), cut into small pieces (cod, seabass, monkfish)
1/2 lb Clams, soaked for 1 hour and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add leeks, onions, celery, and garlic. Cook until the onions are clear.
2. Stir in mushrooms and cook for 1-2 minutes more.
3. Add fish stock (or water), tomato sauce, and white wine. Bring to a gentle simmer.
4. Taste and adjust seasoning with cayenne pepper and salt as needed.
5. Gently add the seafood pieces into the pot, starting with items that require longer cooking times (such as mussels).
6. Simmer until all shellfish are heated through—about 10-15 minutes total.
7. Garnish with chopped parsley before serving in bowls over spaghetti. For a heartier meal, include an additional portion of fish per person.
